name: b-bugtracker

metadata.name: b-bugtracker
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)

documentation:
b, A distributed bug tracker
========================================================================================================================
This version of `b` was forked from [foss.heptapod.net](https://foss.heptapod.net/mercurial/b).  Originally with only minor modifications, but now it's likely safer to say that this tool simply took inspiration from B.

So, with that stated, full credit for the inspiration for this tool goes to Michael Diamond.  Thank you for taking the time and investing the effort to create `b`, without which this tool I love wouldn't likely exist.

The original purpose of B was to serve as a low-feature stand-in for a real, convoluted bug tracking system.  I loved that concept, but have taken the tool much further; much beyond the original, modest scope to the point of adding many features and capabilities to rival those of a fully fledged bug tracking system, but still in a distributed package.

- Now a standalone command-line tool with no dependency upon Mercurial allowing it to be used with any VCS.
    - See the available [commands](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/commands/index) for more info.
- Supports Rich output to make interacting a bit more friendly.
- Supports bug templates to offer a better starting point for new bugs.
    - Also supports customization at the project level - see [templates](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/commands/templates) for more info.
- Handles it's own configuration, outside of Mercurial, in support of independence.
    - See [config command](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/commands/config) for more info about the available configuration options and config file location.

See the [installation](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/installation) and [getting started](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/getting_started) guides for help with installing and using `b` on your project.




Introduction
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
`b` is a tool for tracking bugs and open issues that works with any distributed version control system.  Bugs are tracked as YAML files (i.e., nearly plain text) directly in the `.bugs` directory of the project.  That means that when a user adds a new bug they will need to add it into the VCS and commit it.  Then, all changes made to the bug during the process of diagnosis and resolution will be tracked.

The use of YAML files means that bugs can be opened directly in an editor and manually edited.  In fact, `b` itself does not the ability to set many of the attributes in the bug files from the command line.  It is expected that users will manually open bugs (optionally using the `edit` command) and edit their contents directly.  For more info about the format of these YAML files and the supporting schema, have a look at [the bug file format](https://jwjulien.github.io/b/bugs).




Some Suggested Use Cases
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Small scripts and tasks deserve version control, even if they're never going to be distributed elsewhere.  This is easy with Mercurial.  With `b` installed you get a fully functional bug tracker along with your VCS, no additional setup required! As soon as you install `b`, every repository on your machine now has issue tracking functionality ready to use.

Working on a project with a few other team members is ideal for `b`, it's powerful enough to let everyone track what they need to do, and allow everyone to contribute what they can to any of the bugs on file.  They can search titles for matching bugs, and even grep through the details directory to find details matching what they're looking for.

classes.name: b.exceptions.Error
documentation: Base class for exceptions in this module.

classes.name: b.exceptions.NotInitialized
documentation: Raised when the bugs directory couldn't be found for the current working directory.

classes.name: b.exceptions.AlreadyInitialized
documentation: Raise when init is called but the bugs directory already exists.

classes.name: b.exceptions.UnknownPrefix
documentation: Raised when trying to use a prefix that does not match any tasks.

classes.name: b.exceptions.AmbiguousPrefix
documentation: Raised when trying to use a prefix that could identify multiple tasks.

classes.name: b.exceptions.AmbiguousUser
documentation: Raised when trying to use a prefix that could identify multiple users.

classes.name: b.exceptions.UnknownUser
documentation: Raised when trying to use a user prefix that does not match any users.

classes.name: b.exceptions.UnknownCommand
documentation: Raised when trying to run an unknown command.

classes.name: b.exceptions.InvalidCommand
documentation: Raised when command invocation is invalid, e.g. incorrect options.

classes.name: b.exceptions.InvalidInput
documentation:
Raised when the input to a command is somehow invalid - for example, a username with a | character will cause
problems parsing the bugs file.

classes.name: b.exceptions.TemplateError
documentation: Raised when the specified template does not exist.

modules.name: b.__main__
documentation: Entry point for b bug tracker.

modules.name: b.templates
documentation: Subcommands for modifying custom templates within projects.

functions.name: b.templates.templates
documentation: Configure the bug templates available to this project.

functions.name: b.templates.list
documentation: List the templates that are available to the `add` command.

functions.name: b.templates.customize
documentation: Customize the TEMPLATE for this project.

functions.name: b.migrations.details_to_markdown
documentation: Migrate the details files from .txt format to .md format and swap the headers inside each.

functions.name: b.migrations.details_to_yaml
documentation:
Migrate the details files from Markdown format to YAML format.

The section headings become keys and the section content becomes string values.

functions.name: b.migrations.move_details_to_bugs_root
documentation: Relocate the details files from the "details" subdirectory into the root of the .bugs folder.

functions.name: b.migrations.bug_dict_into_yaml_details
documentation: Migrate the details from the dugs dictionary file into the individual bugs YAML files.

classes.name: b.settings.InvalidSetting
documentation: Raised when the provided key is not accepted/used by this application.

methods.name: b.settings.Settings._split_key
documentation: Split a key name formatted as `section.option` into its constituent parts.

functions.name: b.config.unset
documentation:
Remove the saved setting identified by KEY.

This restores the setting to it's default value.

To list the current settings, issue the "config list" command.

functions.name: b.config.set
documentation: Set the setting identified by KEY to the provided VALUE.

functions.name: b.config.get
documentation: Get the current value for the setting identified by KEY.

functions.name: b.config.list
documentation: List all of the currently configured settings.

functions.name: b.command.init
documentation: Initialize a bugs directory for new bugs.

functions.name: b.command.add
documentation:
Add a new, open bug to the tracker.

TITLE specifies the short summary text to to serve as a title for the bug.

The `template` can be specified using the '-t' or '--template' option.  The default template is "bug".  A complete
list of available templates can be found using the "template list" command.

functions.name: b.command.rename
documentation: Change the title of the bug denoted by PREFIX to the new TITLE.

functions.name: b.command.users
documentation:
Display a list of all users and the number of open bugs assigned to each.

By default, only the count of bugs for each owner are shown.  Use the '-d' flag to list individual bugs for each
user if more information is desired.

By default, only open bugs are displayed for each user.  To list resolved bugs instead, use the '-r' option or to
list all bugs (both open and resolved) use the '-a' switch.

functions.name: b.command.assign
documentation:
Assign bug denoted by PREFIX to USERNAME.

USERNAME can be specified as "nobody" to remove ownership of the bug.

The USERNAME can be a prefix of any username that is enough to uniquely identify an existing user.  For example,
providing a USERNAME of "mi" would be enough to identify a "michael" from a project where "michael" and "mark" are
existing users.  If you would like to assign a new user explicitly without this prefix-matching functionality use
the '-f' flag to force the assignment instead.

functions.name: b.command.details
documentation: Print the extended details of the bug specified by PREFIX.

functions.name: b.command.edit
documentation: Launch the system editor to provide additional details.

functions.name: b.command.comment
documentation: Append the provided COMMENT to the details of the bug identified by PREFIX.

functions.name: b.command.resolve
documentation: Mark the bug identified by PREFIX as resolved.

functions.name: b.command.reopen
documentation: Mark the bug identified by PREFIX as open.

functions.name: b.command.list
documentation: List all bugs according to the specified filters.

functions.name: b.command.id
documentation: Print the full ID of the buf identified by PREFIX.

functions.name: b.command.verify
documentation: Verify that all bug YAML files are valid and report any discrepancies.

functions.name: b.command.migrate
documentation: Migrate bugs directory to the latest version.

functions.name: b.command.version
documentation: Output the version information and exit.

modules.name: b.bugs
documentation: b's business logic and programatic API.

classes.name: b.bugs.Tracker

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.__init__
documentation: Initialize by reading the task files, if they exist.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker._write
documentation: Flush the finished and unfinished tasks to the files on disk.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker._get_bug_path
documentation: Returns the directory and file path to the details specified by id.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker._users_list
documentation: Returns a mapping of usernames to the number of open bugs assigned to that user.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker._get_user
documentation:
Given a user prefix, returns the appropriate username, or fails if the correct user cannot be identified.

'me' is a special username which maps to the username specified when constructing the Bugs.  'Nobody' (and
prefixes of 'Nobody') is a special username which maps internally to the empty string, indicating no assignment.
If force is true, the user 'Nobody' is used.  This is unadvisable, avoid forcing the username 'Nobody'.

If force is true, it assumes user is not a prefix and should be assumed to exist already.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker._launch_editor
documentation:
Open the specified file in the editor specified by the user.

Arguments:
    path: The path to the file to be edited.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.initialize
documentation: Initialize a new bugs directory at the current working directory.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.list_templates
documentation:
Return a dictionary of available templates that can be used to create new bugs.

Arguments:
    only_defaults: Indicates that only the default templates from within the b tool should be returned - none of
        the custom templates from the .bugs directory.

Returns:
    A dictionary where keys correspond to the names of the available templates and the values are the paths to
    the corresponding template file(s).

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.customize_template
documentation: Copy the specified template from the tool directory into the project '.bugs' directory and open the editor.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.edit_template
documentation:
Open the specified custom template for editing.

Arguments:
    template: The CUSTOM template name to be edited.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.id
documentation: Given a prefix, returns the full id of that bug.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.add
documentation:
Adds a new bug to the list.

Arguments:
    title: Title text giving a summarized description of this new bug.
    template: Name of the template file (can be found using the `templates` command) to use for this new bug.
    self_owner: True if the current user should be set at the owner for this new bug.  If False, then no owner
        will be assigned.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.rename
documentation:
Renames the bug.

If more than one task matches the prefix an AmbiguousPrefix exception will be raised, unless the prefix is the
entire ID of one task.

If no tasks match the prefix an UnknownPrefix exception will be raised.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.assign
documentation:
Specifies a new owner of the bug.  Tries to guess the correct user, or warns if it cannot find an appropriate
user.

Using the -f flag will create a new user with that exact name, it will not try to guess, or warn the user.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.edit
documentation: Allows the user to edit the details of the specified bug

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.comment
documentation:
Allows the user to add a comment to the bug without launching an editor.

If they have a username set, the comment will show who made it.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.reopen
documentation: Reopens a bug that was previously resolved

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.details
documentation:
Provides additional details on the requested bug.

Metadata (like owner, and creation time) which are not stored in the details file are displayed along with the
details.

Sections with no content are not displayed.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.users
documentation: Prints bugs grouped by their current owner.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.list
documentation: Lists all bugs, applying the given filters

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.verify
documentation: Verify that each individual bugs file in the bugs folder matches the JSON schema and print any errors.

methods.name: b.bugs.Tracker.migrate
documentation: Migrate the current bugs directory to the latest version.

A set of bugs, issues, and tasks, both finished and unfinished, for a given repository.

The list's file is read from disk when initialized. The items can be written back out to disk with the write()
function.

You can specify any taskdir you want, but the intent is to work from the cwd and therefore anything calling this
class ought to handle that change (normally to the repo root)
